chunk_summary: This section outlines the workflow for catch-up immunization referrals during follow-up for moderate wasting, emphasizing integration with dietary therapy and supplementary feeding programs for children under 5 years of age.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of catch-up immunization during moderate wasting follow-up.
Learn referral workflows for immunization in malnourished children under 5 years.
Integrate dietary therapy with immunization schedules for effective management of moderate wasting.
key_facts: Moderate wasting is managed through dietary therapy and supplementary feeding programs.
Catch-up immunization is critical for children under 5 years to prevent vaccine-preventable diseases.
India's National Immunization Schedule provides guidelines for age-specific vaccinations.
Mission Indradhanush targets full immunization coverage for children and pregnant women.
IMNCI guidelines recommend integrated management of malnutrition and immunization for children aged 2 months to 5 years.
reference_quote: Mission Indradhanush aims to achieve full immunization coverage for all children under 2 years and pregnant women, addressing gaps in routine immunization services. (Source: Mission Indradhanush Guidelines)
document_key_topics: Integration of dietary therapy with immunization schedules
Guidelines for child health programs in India
